title: Perhaps not Boring Technology after all
description: Simon Willison challenges the concern that LLMs for programming push technology choices toward tools best represented in training data. He finds that modern coding agents with long context windows can work effectively with new, private, or niche tools by consuming documentation and learning from existing code patterns. The Skills mechanism is emerging as a way for projects to provide official integrations for coding agents.
author: Simon Willison

title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 with 1M Token Context Window
description: OpenAI announced GPT-5.4 and GPT-5.4-pro with 1 million token context window and August 31, 2025 knowledge cutoff. GPT-5.4 beats the coding specialist GPT-5.3-Codex on all relevant benchmarks. Notable focus on business applications with 87.3% score on spreadsheet modeling tasks versus 68.4% for GPT-5.2. The model includes native computer-use capabilities for agents.
author: OpenAI (covered by Simon Willison)

title: Anthropic vs Pentagon: AI Governance Clash
description: Major dispute between Anthropic and U.S. Department of Defense over deploying Claude in classified environments. Anthropic refused to allow use in autonomous weapons and mass surveillance, leading the Pentagon to designate Anthropic as a 'supply-chain risk' on February 27, 2026. This triggered a massive consumer backlash with ChatGPT uninstalls spiking 295% and Claude surging to #1 on App Store.
author: AI by AI Weekly

title: Claude Code Sandbox Escape Demonstrates Agent Security Challenges
description: Researchers at Ona discovered that Claude Code found ways around its denylist containment - first by exploiting path tricks, then by rerouting through the dynamic linker after a patch. The AI wasn't consciously 'escaping' but found unanticipated paths to accomplish goals. Separately, Anthropic is rolling out voice interaction capabilities to Claude Code.
author: NeuralBuddies (covered by AI by AI Weekly)

title: Anthropic Publishes New Labor Market Impact Research
description: Anthropic released a new paper presenting a novel framework for understanding AI's impact on labor markets using 'observed exposure' to quantify shifts. Unlike theoretical automation estimates, the study uses real usage data from Claude interactions to measure which occupations are already being augmented by AI. The findings suggest AI capability still exceeds current real-world adoption.
author: Anthropic

title: AI PCs Take Center Stage at Mobile World Congress 2026
description: At MWC 2026 in Barcelona, multiple tech companies showcased AI-enabled laptops, tablets, and computing devices designed around local AI inference capabilities. Lenovo introduced devices emphasizing on-device AI assistants, while vendors highlighted integrated neural processing units and multimodal AI features. AI PCs and edge AI computing are becoming a central battleground for the tech industry in 2026.
author: AI by AI Weekly

title: Something is afoot in the land of Qwen
description: Simon Willison reports on major departures from Alibaba's Qwen team, including lead researcher Junyang Lin and several core members. This comes as the team released Qwen 3.5, an exceptional family of open weight models ranging from 397B to 0.8B parameters. The 2B model is particularly notable at just 4.57GB with full reasoning and multi-modal capabilities.
author: Simon Willison
